page: Amazon Web Services r5d.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. The system includes 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Additionally, the instance is equipped with 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score both fall within the average range. However, single-core performance is a relative bottleneck, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks register in the middle 50%. For specialized workloads, Redis SET operations and PassMark database operations also demonstrate average performance. Large-scale LLM inference shows limitations, with text generation for small models and prompt processing for large models scoring in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5d.12xlarge offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its compute resources. The inclusion of 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external block storage for high-throughput local scratch space. However, the tradeoff lies in its single-core processing capabilities, which may limit performance in single-threaded applications.

This instance configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications that require fast local storage, such as relational databases, in-memory caches like Redis, data analytics, and batch processing workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services r5d.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, 1800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance across standard benchmarks, though single-core performance represents a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio and integrated local NVMe storage provide qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads. This instance is configured for memory-intensive applications requiring high-speed local scratch space, such as database operations, in-memory caching, and data processing tasks.

tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-capacity DDR4 RAM, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for data-intensive workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio
1800 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local scratch space
